ReasonCode.description: Canonical held-cycle reason code — a local copy of the optimizer's single-source-of-truth table (optimizer interfaces §Escalation reason codes), kept as a copy per this contract's self-contained convention. Required on an escalated outcome; the SPA renders it plus its class as a ReasonCodeChip.

ReasonClass.description: Operator-triage hint for a reason code: transient may clear on the next cycle once inputs/twin/clock/backend recover; persistent is a deployment/contract/model/bounds fault that will not self-heal (optimizer interfaces).

OptimizerOutcome.description: What the gates decided for one cycle (optimizer plan contract §3 PlanRecord.outcome). `reason_code` is present (required upstream) when status = escalated.

status.description: applied = the immediate bundle was written; escalated = held for operator review; extended = no new plan/write, the last applied bundle stays in force.

reason_code.description: The canonical reason code; present when status = escalated, otherwise null/absent.

OptimizerBackend.description: Which model and prompt produced the plan (optimizer plan contract §3 PlanRecord.backend) — the provenance stamped on every plan.

provider.description: The LLM provider — fixed per optimizer instance (an offline config change), read-only in the console.

model.description: The active model id (e.g. qwen2.5:7b, claude-sonnet-4-6) — operator-mutable within the provider's allowlist.

role.description: Whether this was the primary backend or a fallback (a fallback is a different model held to its own evaluation baseline).

OptimizerPlan.description: The planner's proposed plan (optimizer plan contract §2 OptimizerPlan), flattened for display. Null on a pre-planner held cycle (the enclosing OptimizerPlanView carries the outcome).

confidence.description: The planner's self-assessed confidence in the immediate bundle; below the optimizer's threshold the plan is escalated.

explanation.description: Short natural-language reasoning summary surfaced to the operator.

immediate_setpoints.description: The proposed next bundle — the refined targets the optimizer would submit this cadence, as a partial of Setpoints. Equals SetpointDiff.proposed.

objective_scores.description: The planner's self-reported objective weighting — advisory / explainability only, never a gate input.

OptimizerPlanView.description: One greenhouse's latest optimizer cycle, the Go API's flattening of the optimizer's PlanRecord (optimizer plan contract §3). `plan` is null on a held cycle that ran before the planner; an applied record always carries a non-null plan.

created_at.description: When the cycle ran (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

horizon.description: The adaptive planning window the service chose (12 h default, 24 h near a day boundary).

plan.description: The proposed plan, or null on a pre-planner held cycle.

FieldBound.description: The crop-safe range for one scalar setpoint field, used by the diff to flag a proposed value near or at a bound.

SetpointDiff.description: The Go-API-composed diff the plan panel renders (frontend data model §Optimizer plans): the plan's proposed patch against the greenhouse's current bundle and its crop-safe bounds — both platform-owned, composed here so the SPA reads one response instead of stitching sources.

bounds.description: Crop-safe range per scalar setpoint field (keys are Setpoints field names, e.g. temperature_day_c). The SPA flags a proposed value at/near a bound.

OptimizerPlanDetail.description: The GET /api/optimizer/greenhouses/{id}/plan response — the latest plan view plus its composed setpoint diff. `diff` is null when the plan is null (a held cycle produced no proposed bundle).

diff.description: The proposed-vs-current-vs-bounds diff, or null on a held cycle with no proposed bundle.

Escalation.description: One held cycle awaiting (or, once resolved, having had) operator review. GET /api/optimizer/escalations returns the open set (resolution null); a resolve response echoes it with resolution set.

id.description: Escalation identity (stable across the open→closed transition).

optimizer_run_id.description: The held cycle's run id — links to its PlanRecord/plan view.

created_at.description: When the cycle was held (RFC 3339 UTC, ms).

message.description: Operator-facing detail for why the cycle was held.

resolution.description: How the escalation closed — null while open; operator (resolved here), superseded (a newer cycle produced a fresh outcome), or expired (TTL). Distinct from reason_code (why it was raised).

FleetOptimizerSummary.description: The GET /api/optimizer/fleet rollup for the console overview — per-greenhouse latest outcome plus site aggregates, computed by the optimizer over its plan store so the SPA reads one endpoint instead of fanning out per greenhouse (optimizer interfaces).

status.description: The greenhouse's latest cycle outcome status.

reason_code.description: The reason code when the latest cycle escalated, else null.

enabled.description: Whether the optimizer is enabled for this greenhouse — the per-greenhouse operator pause (POST /api/optimizer/greenhouses/{id}/enabled). false = this greenhouse's scheduled cycles are skipped even while the service is globally enabled; the SPA shows a Disabled pill (overriding `status`). Independent of the global EnableState — effective planning requires both, and the global pause takes precedence.

backlog.description: Open-escalation count (the same scalar GET /health reports).

by_outcome.description: Count of greenhouses by their latest cycle outcome.

oldest_open_age_secs.description: Age of the oldest open escalation in seconds, or null when the backlog is empty.

ModelState.description: The active planning backend and the active provider's allowlist (GET /api/optimizer/model). The ModelSelector offers `available_models`; `provider` is read-only (an offline change).

provider.description: The active provider — fixed per instance.

model.description: The active model id (operator-mutable within available_models).

available_models.description: The active provider's runtime allowlist — the only models the operator may select.

ModelSelection.description: POST /api/optimizer/model body — switch the active model within the allowlist. Takes effect on the next cycle; a model not in available_models is rejected 400.

model.description: The model id to activate — must be in the active provider's available_models.

reason.description: Optional operator note, structured-logged with the change.

EnableState.description: Whether planning is enabled or the optimizer is in read-only mode (GET /api/optimizer/enabled).

enabled.description: true = scheduled cycles run and setpoints may be written; false = read-only mode (planning paused, reads still served).

EnableRequest.description: POST /api/optimizer/enabled and POST /api/optimizer/greenhouses/{id}/enabled body — pause or resume the optimizer at runtime, service-wide or for one greenhouse. In-memory override; resets to the configured default on restart.

enabled.description: Target state: false pauses (read-only), true resumes.

reason.description: Optional operator note, structured-logged with the change.

GreenhouseEnableState.description: Whether the optimizer is enabled for one greenhouse (GET/POST /api/optimizer/greenhouses/{id}/enabled) — the per-greenhouse analog of EnableState. A runtime, in-memory operator pause scoped to a single greenhouse, independent of the service-wide EnableState. Effective planning for a greenhouse requires both this and the global enable; the global pause takes precedence.

enabled.description: true = this greenhouse's scheduled cycles run (subject to the global enable and the usual gates); false = the optimizer skips this greenhouse (no scheduled cycles, no setpoint writes for it), even while the service is globally enabled. In-memory override; resets to the configured default on restart.

DegradedReason.description: Service-level degradation cause for OptimizerStatus.degraded_reason. platform_unreachable / llm_unreachable = a dependency the optimizer needs is down; cycle_stalled = enabled but no successful cycle within the expected cadence; cold_start = enabled but no successful cycle yet. Distinct from the per-cycle escalation ReasonCode (why one greenhouse's cycle was held).

OptimizerStatus.description: Service-level optimizer health for the console overview badge (GET /api/optimizer/status). The Go API's proxy/derivation of the optimizer's internal GET /health (optimizer interfaces §Service API endpoints). Polled. Distinct from per-greenhouse cycle outcomes (FleetOptimizerSummary) — this is the planner service as a whole. The open-escalation backlog is not repeated here; it lives on FleetOptimizerSummary.rollup.backlog.

status.description: healthy = planning normally, or intentionally paused (a read-only pause is a healthy, intentional state, not a stall); degraded = a dependency/cadence problem, see degraded_reason; unavailable = the Go API could not reach the optimizer at all — synthesized by the Go API, not the optimizer, so the console still renders a badge instead of a proxy 5xx.

degraded_reason.description: Machine-readable cause when status = degraded; null otherwise.

enabled.description: false = read-only mode (planning paused). Mirrors GET /api/optimizer/enabled, included so the health badge is self-contained.

read_only_reason.description: Why the service is in read-only mode when enabled = false (the operator's supplied pause note, or a config-default marker); null when enabled.

last_successful_cycle_at.description: When the most recent cycle last applied/extended successfully (RFC 3339 UTC, ms); null before the first successful cycle (cold start).

cadence_secs.description: The fixed scheduled planning interval in seconds (optimizer config cycle_interval_minutes × 60), so the badge can flag staleness against the expected schedule.

CycleRequest.description: POST /api/optimizer/greenhouses/{id}/cycles body — trigger an on-demand planning cycle. Bypasses state-change suppression but not the input/safety/application gates.

reason.description: Optional operator note, structured-logged with the request.

ResolveRequest.description: POST /api/optimizer/escalations/{id}/resolve body — resolve an open escalation. The optional note is structured-logged with the operator identity.

reason.description: Optional operator note explaining the resolution.

CycleAccepted.description: 202 acknowledgement that an on-demand cycle was accepted for dispatch; the resulting plan is fetched from the plan endpoint once the cycle completes.

optimizer_run_id.description: The run id assigned to the triggered cycle.
